title: Maggie Gyllenhaal Says ‘The Bride’ Test Screenings Criticized Film’s Sexual Violence and Warner Bros. Boss Told Her ‘You Can’t Have Frankenstein Lick Black Vomit Off the Bride’s Neck’
content:
ByZack Sharf

Digital News Director

Maggie Gyllenhaaljoined The New York Times’“The Interview” podcastto discuss her latest directorial effort, “The Bride” and revealed how the studio test screening process took her to task over the movie’s depiction of violence and sexual violence. Gyllenhaal wrote and directed “The Bride,” a revisionist look at “The Bride of Frankenstein” that stars Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale.

“There’s sexual violence. There’s violence. Because it’s a big studio movie, we tested and tested it,” Gyllenhaal said. “We had big screenings in malls, where people came to see it, which I had never been a part of as an actress or a director before. So fascinating. And one of the things that they brought up was the violence: Is it too violent? And I was talking about it with a girlfriend of mine, who said — and she wasn’t being reductive — ‘I wonder if you had been a man making this movie, if you would have had the same response.'”

Gyllenhaal said Warner Bros “asked to take some” of the violence out of the movie during test screenings, “so what you’re seeing is even a little bit pulled back from what was originally in the movie.” She made it a priority during the movie’s production not to desensitize any of the violence depicted in the movie.

“One of the things that was important to me is that everybody who is killed, is hurt — we, at least for a moment, get to know them,” Gyllenhaal said. “There’s the Stormtrooper version of killing people, where they have white masks on and you don’t know who they are. And then there’s the version where every single death has a consequence and a cost — every single one.”

Gyllenhaal continued: “I [also] want to talk about the sexual violence, because that’s another thing that I have been taken to task for… in the test screenings. I had a couple of women say, ‘I don’t want to see a woman being violated.’ And I think, Ialsodon’t want to see that. And yet that is a major reality in the culture that we’re living in — just in the time I was cutting this movie, how much wildly disturbing brutality against women there has been in the world. And so if we’re going to see it, we need to see it in a way that is very hard to watch, because it is very awful. And if you know anything about me, if you looked at any of my work, even starting with ‘Secretary’ when I was 22, this is something that I’ve spent a lot of time thinking about. I am sure that I have been thoughtful about this particular subject, and yet it will be hard to watch. I think we can take it.”

“The Bride” is Gyllenhaal’s second feature directorial effort. Her debut was 2021’s “The Lost Daughter,” which earned her an Oscar nomination for best adapted screenplay, plus acting noms for Olivia Colman (best actress) and Jessie Buckley (best supporting actress). With “The Bride,” Gyllenhaal entered the world of studio filmmaking for the first time.

“Yeah, it was difficult, but not in a bad way. It was just very new for me,” Gyllenhaal told The Times. “I loved working with Pam Abdy, who runs Warner Bros. with Mike De Luca. She understood me and understood what I was saying. And there would be times where she would be like: ‘Maggie, you cannot have Frankenstein lick black vomit off the Bride’s neck. It’s just too much. You can’t do it.’ But she understood why I wanted it.”

“The Bride” opens in theaters March 6. Head over toThe New York Times’ websiteto read her full “The Interview” discussion.
title_zh: 瑪姬·吉倫哈爾(Maggie Gyllenhaal)表示，《新娘》(The Bride)的測試放映遭到批評，指責電影中的性暴力，而華納兄弟(Warner Bros.)的負責人告訴她：「你不能讓弗蘭肯斯坦(F Frankenstein)舔掉新娘脖子上的黑色嘔吐物。」
content_zh:
由扎克·沙夫(Zack Sharf)

數位新聞總監

瑪姬·吉倫哈爾(Maggie Gyllenhaal)參加《紐約時報》(The New York Times)的播客《訪談》(The Interview)，討論她最新的導演作品《新娘》(The Bride)，並透露在電影的測試放映過程中，製片廠對電影中暴力和性暴力的描繪提出了質疑。吉倫哈爾編劇並執導了《新娘》，這是一部對《科學怪人的新娘》(The Bride of Frankenstein)的修正主義詮釋，主演包括傑西·巴克利(Jessie Buckley)和克里斯蒂安·貝爾(Christian Bale)。

“裡面有性暴力，有暴力。因為這是一部大型製片廠的電影，我們進行了多次測試，”吉倫哈爾說。“我們在購物中心舉辦了大型放映，觀眾來觀看這部電影，這是我作為演員或導演從未參與過的經歷。非常有趣。其中一個他們提到的問題是暴力：這是否太過暴力？我和一位女性朋友談論這個問題，她說——她並不是在簡化——‘我想知道如果你是一位男性導演拍這部電影，會不會得到相同的反應。’”

吉倫哈爾表示，華納兄弟(Warner Bros)在測試放映期間“要求刪減一些”暴力場面，“所以你所看到的甚至比電影最初的內容稍微減少了一點。”她在電影製作過程中優先考慮不讓任何描繪的暴力變得麻木。

“對我來說重要的一點是，每一個被殺或受傷的人——我們至少在某一刻能夠認識他們，”吉倫哈爾說。“有一種暴力的表現，就像風暴兵一樣，他們戴著白色面具，你不知道他們是誰。然後還有一種表現，每一個死亡都有後果和代價——每一個。”

吉倫哈爾接著說：“我[也]想談談性暴力，因為這是我在測試放映中被質疑的另一件事。我有幾位女性說，‘我不想看到一位女性被侵犯。’我想，我也不想看到那樣。然而，這卻是我們所生活文化中的一個重大現實——在我剪輯這部電影的期間，世界上對女性的極度暴力事件層出不窮。因此，如果我們要看到這些，我們需要以一種非常難以觀看的方式來呈現，因為這真的很可怕。如果你對我有任何了解，看看我過去的作品，甚至從我22歲時的《秘書》(Secretary)開始，這是我花了很多時間思考的主題。我相信我對這個特定主題是經過深思熟慮的，但這將會是難以觀看的。我認為我們可以承受。”

《新娘》是吉倫哈爾的第二部長片導演作品。她的首部作品是2021年的《失落的女兒》(The Lost Daughter)，該片為她贏得了最佳改編劇本的奧斯卡提名，並為奧利維亞·科爾曼(Olivia Colman)（最佳女主角）和傑西·巴克利（最佳女配角）獲得了演技提名。隨著《新娘》，吉倫哈爾首次進入了大型製片廠的電影製作領域。

“是的，這很困難，但並不是壞事。對我來說，這只是非常新鮮，”傑克·吉倫哈爾(Jake Gyllenhaal)告訴《泰晤士報》。 “我喜歡與帕姆·阿比(Pam Abdy)合作，她與邁克·德盧卡(Mike De Luca)一起管理華納兄弟(Warner Bros.)。她理解我，也理解我在說什麼。有時她會說：‘瑪姬(Maggie)，你不能讓弗蘭肯斯坦舔新娘的脖子上的黑色嘔吐物。這實在太過了。你不能這樣做。’但她理解我為什麼想這樣做。”

《新娘》(The Bride)將於3月6日上映。請前往《紐約時報》(The New York Times)的網站閱讀她的完整“訪談”(The Interview)討論。

由扎克·沙夫(Zack Sharf)

數位新聞總監
